ogTitle: Sandy Point vs Sargood: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sandy Point and Sargood.
heading: Sandy Point vs Sargood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sandy Point, Victoria and Sargood, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sargood has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Sandy Point (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sandy Point or Sargood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sandy Point has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Sargood scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sandy Point scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Sargood scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sandy Point or Sargood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sargood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
